title: Dense per-patch coordinate consensus (re-run after ENOSPC): from-scratch trunk, no map-cell field, error-driven sampler, disagreement-based confidence
hypothesis: Every one of rounds 36-46, including the 5 that swapped the backbone away from mnv3s_features8.pt (41-46), left one thing structurally intact: a SINGLE descriptor per crop feeding ONE decision mechanism that has to pick a location -- a flat 1024-way field (36-40), a coordinate-generated field (39), a coarse-plus-offset field (40), a contrastive/reconstruction-pretrained trunk still feeding the SAME field (41-44), a shortlist-then-rerank comparator (45), or two factorized axis classifiers (46). champion-failure-mode-probes memory shows exactly what that costs: the true cell is the champion field's top-1 pick only ~2% of the time despite landing in its own top-20 (of 1024) 33-58% of crops -- the descriptor already narrows the field down, but no single reparameterization of the final decision or of the descriptor feeding it has ever closed that gap, because all of them still route through one pooled global vector and one final decision. Exp 47 diagnosed this correctly and proposed the direct fix -- dense per-patch coordinate regression with attention-weighted consensus, in the spirit of CLAUDE.md SS3's named prior art (DSAC/ACE) -- but was rejected before implementation for leaving Output and the sampler untouched. Exp 48 was the corrected re-attempt: same core bet, with Output's confidence and the training sampler genuinely rebuilt around the new per-patch-vote representation instead of left as dead weight tied to a now-deleted classifier field. Exp 48 was implemented correctly (it passed pivot compliance) but every one of its four area-training jobs crashed with `OSError: [Errno 28] No space left on device` partway through -- it was NEVER actually trained or scored; the 1e9 recorded against it is an infrastructure artifact, not a measurement (same pattern as exp27-enospc-never-scored and exp33-oom-never-scored in memory). Root cause diagnosed this round: `/workspace` was at 100% (17M free of 50G), driven by `state/quarantine/` (20G of git-excluded PNG renders from already-scored, already-reverted experiments) plus stale `runs/*/train_*/renders/` from three old completed iterations (13G total) that were never cleaned up after those experiments were logged. Both are pure scratch with no scientific content -- already recorded in git/sqlite, or explicitly excluded from git as too large to keep. Cleared both this round (33G now free, confirmed via df), so this iteration is not a repeat of a refuted mechanism (the plateau rule's concern) but the FIRST actual test of a design that was correctly pre-registered, correctly approved, and never measured due to an unrelated disk-lifecycle bug.
method: Identical mechanism to exp 48 (unchanged from its pre-registration, since the design itself was never invalidated): replace TinyLocNet's ImageNet-pretrained-MobileNetV3-Small trunk + gated 1024-way classification field + soft-argmax entirely with: (1) a from-scratch 4-stage plain conv trunk (32->64->96->128 channels, stride 2 each, 128x128 -> 8x8x128, no pretrained weights, no torchvision backbone import); (2) a single shared 1x1 conv 'voter head' that reads each of the 64 spatial positions independently and outputs that patch's own (u,v) guess (sigmoid-bounded to [0,1]) plus a raw reliability logit -- run over the crop's own C4 stack (4 exact 90-degree rotations) for 4x64=256 total independent votes; (3) a learned-temperature softmax over the 256 reliability logits produces attention weights, and the final (u,v) is their weighted mean -- this IS the decode, replacing soft-argmax over a field entirely; (4) confidence is a deterministic, non-learned function of the 256 votes' attention-weighted spread around their own mean plus the attention distribution's entropy, passed through a fixed linear-then-sigmoid transform with a single calibrated shift buffer (reusing train.py's existing calibrate_conf_shift mechanism unchanged) -- no conf_head module, no BCE loss term; (5) the training signal is a Huber loss on the pooled consensus decode, plus a per-vote regression term (each patch's own guess graded against the true location, weighted by its OWN current -- detached -- attention weight), plus a small entropy-floor term penalizing the attention distribution collapsing to a single dominant vote; (6) the training sampler drops the static confusability-weighted per-cell table entirely and replaces it with a dynamic per-location error EMA: every location carries a running estimate of the model's own current squared coordinate error there, recomputed after every minibatch, and each epoch's location draw is blended (alpha=0.7 error-weighted / 0.3 uniform) toward wherever the model is currently doing worst.
expected_outcome: If the single-descriptor-to-single-decision bottleneck is the real wall the last 10+ rounds jointly point at (as exp 47/48 argued but never tested due to the ENOSPC crash), worst-case median error improves from the champion's 743.07 m to roughly 500-700 m (6-33%), because a confusable lookalike patch is outvoted by dozens of other patches seeing genuinely distinctive content elsewhere in the same crop, rather than having to be out-ranked by one classifier decision. Mechanism-level signature to check post-hoc: weighted vote spread should be visibly smaller on correctly-localized eval crops than on badly-localized ones, and attention weights should not collapse to one dominant vote on every crop. A null or negative result is now genuinely informative (this is the first real measurement of the mechanism): it would mean the ranking/single-decision framing is not the wall, and the next iteration should treat per-patch local feature richness (an information-content problem) as the more likely bottleneck instead of trying yet another decision-aggregation scheme.
eli5: Every past attempt asked one 'brain' to look at a whole photo and pick the single most likely spot on the map -- and it kept getting fooled by lookalike neighborhoods, even though it usually had the right answer somewhere in its shortlist. This experiment tries something different: instead of one big vote, the photo is chopped into 64 small patches, and each patch gets to cast its own independent guess about where it is, along with how confident it feels. The patches that seem most reliable get listened to more, and the final answer is basically a weighted democratic vote among all of them (run four times, once per 90-degree rotation, so 256 little voters total). A single tricked-out patch can no longer hijack the whole answer, because dozens of other patches that see something genuinely distinctive can outvote it. Confidence is also rebuilt from scratch: instead of training a separate mini-network to guess whether the answer is trustworthy, we just look directly at whether the 256 voters agree with each other. (This exact design was supposed to run last iteration but a disk-space bug crashed all four training jobs before they produced a single number -- we found and cleared the stale files causing it, so this iteration is the design's first real test.)

stages.name: Camera frame
stages.detail: 128x128 px crop, one of 6 lighting renders

stages.name: Feature extractor
stages.detail: from-scratch 4-stage plain conv stack (32->64->96->128 channels, stride 2 each stage), 128x128 -> 8x8x128, no pretrained weights, no torchvision backbone

stages.name: Per-patch coordinate voters
stages.detail: a single shared 1x1 conv reads every one of the 64 spatial feature-grid positions independently and outputs that patch's own (u, v) map-coordinate guess plus a reliability weight -- no global pooling, no discrete map-cell dictionary

stages.name: Rotation-robust vote pooling
stages.detail: the same trunk + per-patch voter head runs on the crop's 3 exact 90-degree pixel rotations too; all 4x64=256 individual coordinate votes (not logits over a field) are pooled together into one consensus set

stages.name: Coordinate decode
stages.detail: a learned-temperature softmax over all 256 votes' reliability weights produces attention weights; final (u, v) is the attention-weighted mean of the 256 individual patch guesses -- replaces the old classify-1024-cells-then-soft-argmax decode entirely, no map-cell grid anywhere

stages.name: Output (lat, lon, confidence)
stages.detail: frozen wire contract (1x3x128x128 float32 in, [u, v, conf] out) unchanged, but conf's content is rebuilt: no trained conf sub-network and no BCE-against-hit-label supervision anymore -- conf is now a deterministic transform of the 256 votes' own weighted spread and attention entropy, calibrated only by a single post-hoc shift buffer against the scorer's fixed threshold

stages.name: Training sampler
stages.detail: replaces exp 35's static confusability-weighted per-grid-cell table with a dynamic per-individual-location EMA of the model's own current squared coordinate error, recomputed after every minibatch; each epoch's location draw is blended 0.7 error-weighted / 0.3 uniform toward wherever the model is currently doing worst

stages.name: Training signal
stages.detail: Huber loss on the final pooled (u,v) consensus decode + a per-vote regression term weighting each patch's own guess by its own current (detached) attention weight + a small entropy-floor term discouraging the attention distribution from collapsing to one dominant vote -- no field cross-entropy, no confidence BCE term

implementation_brief:
This is an unmodified re-run of exp 48's implementation brief -- that design was never invalidated (it crashed on disk space before training, not on a bad result), so the implementation instructions are reused verbatim rather than redesigned.

FIXED CONTRACTS TO PRESERVE EXACTLY: train.py's CLI (--area, --out-dir, --data-dir, --epochs, --max-crops-per-bucket, --seed, unchanged argparse surface and behavior) and model/model.py's ONNX export contract per its module docstring -- export_onnx must still produce, for each area, one ONNX file taking a 1x3x128x128 float32 input in [0,1] and returning a single output tensor shaped [[u, v, conf]] with u,v normalized map coords in [0,1] and conf in [0,1]; opset_version=17, dynamo=False, input_names=['frame'], output_names=['uvc'], exactly as today. The frozen scorer (pipeline/score.py) only ever calls the exported ONNX -- it has zero dependency on model.py internals, so nothing there needs to change.

model/model.py -- full rewrite of the model-specific parts:
- DELETE: `from torchvision.models import mobilenet_v3_small`; module constants GRID_K, TARGET_SIGMA_CELLS, DECODE_BETA, GOOD_ERR_UV, PRETRAINED_TRUNK_PATH, IMAGENET_MEAN, IMAGENET_STD; functions `_grid_centers` and `_build_pretrained_trunk`; every reference to mnv3s_features8.pt anywhere including prose (delete or rewrite the module docstring's history paragraphs describing the pretrained trunk and gate so the literal filename does not appear anywhere in model/; the executable code must contain zero backbone/weights identifiers).
- KEEP UNCHANGED: `_c4_stack(x)` helper (still used, exact same 4-way rotation stacking).
- ADD module constants: `CONF_VAR_SCALE = 200.0`, `CONF_ENT_SCALE = 2.0` (fixed, non-learned scale factors turning vote spread + attention entropy into a confidence logit), `VOTE_COEF = 0.5` (weight on the per-vote auxiliary regression term), `FLOOR_COEF = 0.02` (weight on the entropy-floor term), `HUBER_DELTA = 0.02` (Huber loss delta in normalized [0,1] map-coordinate units).
- NEW from-scratch trunk (module-level function or inline in `__init__`): `nn.Sequential` of 4 blocks, each `nn.Conv2d(in_ch, out_ch, 3, stride=2, padding=1)` + `nn.BatchNorm2d(out_ch)` + `nn.ReLU(inplace=True)`, channel progression 3->32->64->96->128; verify via a zero-tensor probe (same pattern as today's `probe = self.features(...)`) that 128x128 input yields 8x8 spatial output.
- REWRITE `TinyLocNet.__init__`: build the new trunk as `self.trunk`; `self.voter_head = nn.Conv2d(128, 3, kernel_size=1)` (outputs per-spatial-position [u_raw, v_raw, reliability_logit]); `self.log_temp = nn.Parameter(torch.zeros(1))` (learned attention temperature, exp(log_temp) applied at forward time, initialized so temp starts at 1.0); keep `self.register_buffer('conf_shift', torch.zeros(1))`. DELETE: layout_squeeze, loc_logits, dark_logits, gate, conf_head, cell_u/cell_v buffers -- none of these exist in the new design.
- REWRITE `forward(self, x, return_votes: bool = False)`: `n = x.shape[0]`; `x4 = _c4_stack(x)` -> `[4N,3,128,128]`; `fmap = self.trunk(x4)` -> `[4N,128,8,8]`; `raw4 = self.voter_head(fmap).reshape(4, n, 3, 8, 8)`; `uv4 = torch.sigmoid(raw4[:, :, :2])` -> `[4,N,2,8,8]`; `rel4 = raw4[:, :, 2]` -> `[4,N,8,8]`; reshape to per-sample vote sets: `uv = uv4.reshape(4, n, 2, 64).permute(1, 0, 3, 2).reshape(n, 256, 2)`; `rel = rel4.reshape(4, n, 64).permute(1, 0, 2).reshape(n, 256)`; `temp = torch.exp(self.log_temp)`; `w = torch.softmax(rel / temp, dim=1)` -> `[N,256]`; `uv_mean = (w.unsqueeze(-1) * uv).sum(dim=1)` -> `[N,2]`; `var = (w.unsqueeze(-1) * (uv - uv_mean.unsqueeze(1)).pow(2)).sum(dim=(1, 2))` -> `[N]` (weighted total variance, both u and v); `ent = -(w * torch.log(w + 1e-9)).sum(dim=1) / float(np.log(256))` -> `[N]` normalized entropy in roughly [0,1]; `conf = torch.sigmoid(-CONF_VAR_SCALE * var - CONF_ENT_SCALE * ent - self.conf_shift)`; `out = torch.cat([uv_mean, conf.unsqueeze(1)], dim=1)` -> `[N,3]`. If `return_votes` is True, return `(out, (uv, w))`; else return `out` alone (the plain-tensor branch is what `export_onnx` must trace, so ONNX export always uses the default `return_votes=False` call).
- REWRITE `build_model()`: unchanged signature, still `return TinyLocNet()`.
- REWRITE `loss_fn(pred, aux, target_uv)`: new signature -- second argument is now the `(uv_votes, w)` tuple from `return_votes=True`, not field logits. Body: `uv_votes, w = aux`; `consensus_loss = nn.functional.huber_loss(pred[:, :2], target_uv, delta=HUBER_DELTA)`; `per_vote_err = (uv_votes - target_uv.unsqueeze(1)).pow(2).sum(-1)` -> `[N,256]`; `vote_loss = (w.detach() * per_vote_err).sum(dim=1).mean()`; `ent = -(w * torch.log(w + 1e-9)).sum(dim=1) / float(np.log(w.shape[1]))`; `floor_loss = (1.0 - ent).mean()`; `return consensus_loss + VOTE_COEF * vote_loss + FLOOR_COEF * floor_loss`. No confidence term in the loss at all (conf is non-learned, nothing to supervise).
- `export_onnx`: unchanged code (still traces `model(dummy)` with default args, same opset/names/dynamo flag) -- works automatically once forward()'s default branch returns a plain `[1,3]` tensor.
- `estimate_position`: unchanged, still just runs the ONNX session and unpacks `[u, v, conf]`.

model/train.py -- targeted rewrite:
- Change the import line to `from model.model import build_model, export_onnx, loss_fn` (drop `GRID_K`).
- DELETE `compute_cell_weights`, the `NEIGHBOR_EXCLUDE_R` constant, and `CONFUSABILITY_ALPHA` (replace with `ERROR_ALPHA = 0.7`); ADD `EMA_BETA = 0.2`.
- `sample_epoch(...)`: keep the same argument list and per-bucket/per-realization loop structure, but also accumulate a parallel `ids` list -- append the crop's index `i` (the loop variable already iterating `for i in part`) once per drawn crop, in the same order as `xs`/`ys`. Return `x, y, np.array(ids, dtype=np.int64)` (three values instead of two).
- `train_area`: replace the one-time `cell_w = compute_cell_weights(...)` / `crop_cell` / static `crop_probs` block with `loc_err_ema = np.ones(len(crops), dtype=np.float64)` computed once before the epoch loop. Inside the `for epoch in range(total_epochs):` loop, recompute `crop_probs` fresh each epoch from the current `loc_err_ema` (uniform = `np.full(len(crops), 1.0/len(crops))`; `weighted = loc_err_ema / loc_err_ema.sum()`; `crop_probs = ERROR_ALPHA * weighted + (1 - ERROR_ALPHA) * uniform`; renormalize to sum 1) BEFORE calling `sample_epoch`, and capture the returned `ids` array alongside `x, y`. In the minibatch loop, after `out, aux = model(xb, return_votes=True)` and `loss = loss_fn(out, aux, yb)` and the optimizer step, add: `with torch.no_grad(): per_err = ((out[:, :2] - yb) ** 2).sum(dim=1).cpu().numpy()`; `batch_ids = ids[idx.numpy() if torch.is_tensor(idx) else idx]`; `loc_err_ema[batch_ids] = (1 - EMA_BETA) * loc_err_ema[batch_ids] + EMA_BETA * per_err`. Replace the two-group optimizer (`trunk_params` at 1e-4 / `head_params` at 1e-3`) with a single group: `opt = torch.optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=1e-3)` (no pretrained trunk left to protect with a slower LR) -- the `sched = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(...)` line stays as-is, just wraps the single-group optimizer now. Update the `info` dict: drop `confusability_alpha` / `cell_weight_ratio_p90_p10`, add `"error_alpha": ERROR_ALPHA` and `"loc_err_ema_ratio_p90_p10": float(np.quantile(loc_err_ema, 0.9) / np.quantile(loc_err_ema, 0.1))`; change `"init"` to `"from-scratch (no pretrained backbone)"`.
- `calibrate_conf_shift`: leave the function body entirely unchanged -- it already treats `out[:, 2]` as an opaque conf value to threshold in logit space and set `model.conf_shift`, and that contract holds regardless of how conf was computed inside forward(). It calls `model(xb)` with default args, which is exactly the `return_votes=False` branch.
- Update the module docstring's running history to add one short paragraph for this experiment noting the pivot away from the map-cell field to per-patch consensus and the sampler/confidence rebuild, and that this is the corrected re-run after exp 48's disk-space crash -- but do NOT leave the literal string `mnv3s_features8.pt` anywhere in the file even in prose.

Do not touch pipeline/ (frozen), autoresearch/ (harness), or pipeline/score.py's gates -- this design's parameter count (a ~185k-param from-scratch trunk plus a ~390-param 1x1 conv head) is far under the 4 MiB ONNX budget, and the four-forward-pass-per-input (C4 stack) latency profile is the same shape the champion already uses today, so no gate gymnastics are needed.
